title: Quantum simulates properties of the first-ever half-Möbius molecule, designed by IBM and researchers
why:
- Researchers created the first molecule exhibiting a half-Möbius electronic topology, a novel form of quantum matter not previously conjectured.
- Understanding the molecule's behavior required quantum-centric supercomputing, integrating quantum and classical systems.
- Traditional classical simulation methods (e.g., Quantum Monte Carlo, CASSCF, CCSD) were insufficient due to the molecule's strong electronic correlations and multireference character.
- The team used a quantum algorithm (SqDRIFT) on an IBM Heron quantum processor, leveraging up to 100 qubits to explore electronic structure beyond the reach of classical computation.
- Quantum simulations helped reveal key properties such as the helical pseudo-Jahn-Teller effect and confirmed twisted molecular orbitals, providing a microscopic explanation for experimental observations.
- This work demonstrates quantum computing as a practical scientific instrument in quantum chemistry, addressing real experimental systems beyond theoretical examples.
